---
title: "Picrust_differential_abundance"
author: "Cynthia"
date: "2026-03-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r libraries, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggpicrust2)
library(Maaslin2)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
metadata = read.csv("../../../Datasets/Metadata/parkinsons_metadata_filt_cat.csv", row.names = 1)
metadata_sub = metadata %>% filter(Tert_VC %in% c("VC Low", "VC High")) %>%
  mutate(Tert_VC = fct_drop(as.factor(Tert_VC)))
```

# ggpicrust2 to run "Maaslin2", another common differential abundance tool, on your groups of interest and add pathway annotations
```{r}
meta = readRDS('../../../Datasets/phyloseq_taxonomy.rds') %>% .@sam_data %>% data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column('sample_name')

pathway_abundance = read.delim('../../../QIIME2_outputs/path_abun_unstrat.tsv',row.names = 1)

# Filter
path_filt = pathway_abundance %>% select(all_of(rownames(metadata_sub)))


diff_abun = pathway_daa(abundance = path_filt,
                   metadata = metadata,
                   group = "Tert_VC",
                   daa_method = "Maaslin2",
                   fixed_effects = c("Sex", "Age", "entacapone"),
                   select = NULL,
                   reference = "VC Low")

daa_annotated_results_df = pathway_annotation(pathway = "MetaCyc",
                                              daa_results_df = diff_abun,
                                              ko_to_kegg = FALSE)
 
# save
saveRDS(daa_annotated_results_df,'daa_annotated_results_df.rds')

readRDS('daa_annotated_results_df.rds')

```

# Sanity Check/Manual
```{r}
# STEP 1: Convert your counts to relative abundance

# Calculation must be done PER SAMPLE (column), not per feature (row)!!
path_relab = apply(path_filt, MARGIN = 2, function(x) x/sum(x))
# double check that all counts sum to 1 now
table(colSums(path_relab)) # See the module for why some aren't exactly 1!
# STEP 2: make a data table with all your samples, counts per pathway, and variable of interest.

# First I'll make a 'long' version of the pathway count table
path_long = path_relab %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% # apply() turned it into a matrix, need a data frame
  rownames_to_column('pathway') %>% # move the function names to a column
  pivot_longer(cols = -pathway, names_to = 'sample_name', values_to = 'value')

# Then I'll add my variable of interest
combined_table = path_long %>% left_join(metadata_sub %>% rownames_to_column('sample_name'), by = 'sample_name')
head(combined_table)

# STEP 3: Add Pseudocount

# Very important: later on, we're going to divide by values that might be zero and use log transformations. We need to add a pseudocount to everything first.
# half of the smallest non-zero value in the relative abundance data:
pseudocount_value = min(combined_table$value[combined_table$value>0])/2
pseudocount_value # 5.1e-07, real small

# Then add it to all the abundances:
combined_table$value = combined_table$value + pseudocount_value
min(combined_table$value) # Now equal to the pseudocount!

# STEP 4: for every pathway, calculate the average relative abundance for each group of interest.

# There shouldn't be any NAs, but can include na.rm=T in mean() for safety
# Can use group_modify as well, or other nifty approaches
avg_abundances = combined_table %>% 
  group_by(pathway,Tert_VC) %>% # pathway and variable of interest
  summarize(value = mean(value)) %>% 
  ungroup() # Don't forget to ungroup!

# STEP 5: Take the ratio of your groups of interest, then apply a log2 fold change

# Personally, I'll pivot wider to make it easier
ratio = avg_abundances %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = Tert_VC, values_from = value) %>% 
  mutate(ratio = `VC High`/`VC Low`) %>% # Group of interest / control group, if possible
  mutate(log2fc = log2(ratio)) # Could combine with above step

head (ratio) # If they have NAs, it might be because of their normalization technique (ex. CLR, which generates negative abundance values)

# STEP 6: Filter for log2FC >=1
# ideally this is an absolute log2FC cutoff (<=-1 or >=1), but I didn't specify that so >=1 is fine.
ratio_filt = ratio %>% 
  filter(abs(log2fc)>=1)

# Filter the differential abudnance results to only include those above the log2 fold change cutoff
daa_results_log2fc_filtered = daa_annotated_results_df %>% 
  filter(feature %in% ratio_filt$pathway)

# Adjusted P-value = 37 hits
daa_results_log2fc_filtered %>% 
  filter(p_adjust < 0.05) %>% 
  nrow()

significant_pathways = daa_results_log2fc_filtered %>% 
  filter(p_adjust < 0.05) %>%
  pull(feature)

# save for aim 4
saveRDS(significant_pathways,'VC_significant_pathways.rds')


sanity_peb = pathway_errorbar_fixed(abundance = path_filt, 
                     daa_results_df = daa_results_log2fc_filtered, 
                     Group = metadata_sub$Tert_VC, 
                     wrap_label = F, wraplength = 60,
                     fc_cutoff = 0, order_by_log = F,
                     p_values_threshold = 0.005, 
                     ko_to_kegg = F, 
                     p_value_bar = TRUE, 
                     x_lab = "description")
# Save the final sanity checked version with the tags
ggsave("../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/VC-picrust-sanity-final-with-labels.png", plot = sanity_peb, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

```

Plots
```{r}
source('../ggpicrust2_errorbar_function_fixed.R')

# Filter out NA in p-values and log2 fold change columns
daa_annotated_results_df_filt = daa_annotated_results_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_adjust), !is.na(log2_fold_change))

# Adjusted P-value = 17 hits
daa_annotated_results_df_filt %>% 
  filter(p_adjust < 0.05) %>% 
  nrow()

# filter for only significant pathways
signif_path = daa_results_log2fc_filtered %>% 
  filter(p_adjust < 0.05)

# Plotting all 37 significant pathways as 2 separate plots
daa_part1 <- signif_path[1:18, ]

daa_part2 <- signif_path[19:37, ] # missing names for some
daa_part2$description[10] <- "D-erythronate degradation II"
daa_part2$description[11] <- "L-threonate degradation"
daa_part2$description[12] <- "glycogen biosynthesis III (from α-maltose 1-phosphate)"
daa_part2$description[13] <- "Entner-Doudoroff pathway I"

peb_part1 = pathway_errorbar_fixed(abundance = path_filt,
                                   daa_results_df = daa_part1, 
                                   Group = metadata_sub$Tert_VC, 
                                   wrap_label = T, wraplength = 45,
                                   fc_cutoff = 0,
                                   order_by_log = T,
                                   p_values_threshold = 1, 
                                   ko_to_kegg = FALSE, 
                                   p_value_bar = TRUE, 
                                   x_lab = "description")


peb_part2 = pathway_errorbar_fixed(abundance = path_filt,
                                   daa_results_df = daa_part2, 
                                   Group = metadata_sub$Tert_VC, 
                                   wrap_label = T, wraplength = 45,
                                   fc_cutoff = 0,
                                   order_by_log = T,
                                   p_values_threshold = 1, 
                                   ko_to_kegg = FALSE, 
                                   p_value_bar = TRUE, 
                                   x_lab = "description")

# Save Part 1
ggsave("../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/Vitamin_C/VC-picrust-part1.png",
       plot = peb_part1, width = 18, height = 12)

# Save Part 2
ggsave("../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/Vitamin_C/VC-picrust-part2.png",
       plot = peb_part2, width = 18, height = 12)

# filter for top 10 greatest magnitude changes
top_10 <- ratio %>%
  filter(pathway %in% signif_path$feature)%>%
  arrange(desc(abs(log2fc))) %>%  # Sort by greatest magnitude L2FC
  head(10)

daa_results_top10 = daa_annotated_results_df %>% 
  filter(feature %in% top_10$pathway)

peb10 = pathway_errorbar_fixed(abundance = path_filt, 
                     daa_results_df = daa_results_top10, 
                     Group = metadata_sub$Tert_VC, 
                     wrap_label = F, wraplength = 60,
                     fc_cutoff = 0, order_by_log = F,
                     p_values_threshold = 1, 
                     ko_to_kegg = F, 
                     p_value_bar = TRUE, 
                     x_lab = "description")

ggsave("../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/Vitamin_C/VC-picrust-sig-pathways10.png",
       plot = peb10, width = 18, height = 15, dpi = 300)
```

Heatmap

```{r identical features}
sig_features = signif_path %>% 
  filter(p_adjust < 1, abs(log2_fold_change) > 0) %>% 
  pull('feature') %>% unique()

path_relab = path_filt %>% 
  apply(2,function(x) x/sum(x)) %>% as.data.frame()
colSums(path_relab) # Should be all 1

stats = path_relab %>% t() %>%  # switch rows and columns
  as.data.frame() %>% # Lets us use select()
  select(all_of(sig_features)) %>% 
  cor(method = 'pearson') #Pearson tests between every feature pair

# Define the color palette
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(40)

# Define breaks for the color scale
breaks <- seq(-1, 1, length.out = 41)  # 40 colors + 1 for the endpoint

# Lookup Names
name_lookup <- daa_annotated_results_df %>%
  filter(feature %in% sig_features) %>%
  select(feature, description) %>% # Adjust 'description' to your actual column name
  distinct()

readable_names <- name_lookup$description[match(rownames(stats), name_lookup$feature)]

# manually finding names
readable_names[28] <- "D-erythronate degradation II"
readable_names[29] <- "L-threonate degradation"
readable_names[30] <- "glycogen biosynthesis III (from α-maltose 1-phosphate)"
readable_names[31] <- "Entner-Doudoroff pathway I"

rownames(stats) <- readable_names
colnames(stats) <- readable_names
# Wrap row names (pathway names) at 30 characters
rownames(stats) <- str_wrap(rownames(stats), width = 30)

# Wrap column names at 30 characters
colnames(stats) <- str_wrap(colnames(stats), width = 30)
view(readable_names)

# Create the clustered heatmap with centered color at zero
# install.packages("pheatmap")
library('pheatmap')

# to try to minimize white space
pheatmap(stats, 
         clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",  
         clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean", 
         clustering_method = "complete",          
         color = color_palette, 
         breaks = breaks,
         border_color = "white",
         display_numbers = TRUE,
         number_format = "%.2f",      
         fontsize_row = 10,
         fontsize_col = 10,
         angle_col = 315,              # rotated to minimize white space
         treeheight_row = 25,         
         treeheight_col = 25, 
         legend = TRUE,               
         main = NA,                   # Use NA to properly suppress the title space
         filename = "../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/Vitamin_C/MetaCyc-VC_Heatmap_Final-new.png",
         width = 35,                   # Increased slightly for your long pathway names
         height = 33)

```

See how similar your sample groups are
```{r PCA}
library(ggExtra)

# Generate the base PCA plot WITHOUT the marginals
pca_plot = pathway_pca(abundance = path_filt,
                        metadata = metadata_sub, 
                        group = "Tert_VC",
                        show_marginal = FALSE) # Disable the hardcoded curves

# labels
pca_plot = pca_plot + 
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(color = "Tert_VC")

# Add the marginal density plots manually
# type = "density" creates the curves
# groupFill = TRUE ensures the colors match your groups
# alpha = 0.3 sets the transparency you want
final_plot = ggMarginal(pca_plot, 
                        type = "density", 
                        groupFill = TRUE, 
                        alpha = 0.3)

# 4. Save the final object
ggsave("../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/VC-pathway-pca.png", 
       plot = final_plot, 
       width = 8, 
       height = 6, 
       dpi = 300)
```

Volcano
```{r}
library(ggrepel)
library(stringr)
# 1. Join the p-values from Maaslin2 with the log2FC you calculated
plot_data <- daa_annotated_results_df %>%
  left_join(ratio, by = c("feature" = "pathway")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Define significance categories for coloring
    status = case_when(
      p_adjust < 0.05 & log2fc >= 1 ~ "More Abundant (VC Low)",
      p_adjust < 0.05 & log2fc <= -1 ~ "Less Abundant (VC High)",
      TRUE ~ "Not Significant"
    )
  )

# 1. Create a separate dataframe for just the top 10 hits
# We use slice_min to get the 10 lowest p_adjust values
top_10_labels <- plot_data %>%
  filter(p_adjust < 0.05) %>%      # Only significant hits
  slice_min(p_adjust, n = 10)      # Take the 10 smallest p-values

# 2. Update your volcano plot
volcano_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(p_adjust), color = status)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("More Abundant (VC Low)" = "#D55E00", 
                                "Less Abundant (VC High)" = "#0072B2", 
                                "Not Significant" = "grey70")) +
  
  # Use the new top_10_labels dataframe here
  
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = expression(log[2]~"Fold Change"),
    y = expression(-log[10]~"Adjusted P-value"),
    color = "Significance"
  ) +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# Display and Save
print(volcano_plot)
ggsave("../../../Results/Figures/Aim_3/VC-volcano-plot-top10.png", 
       plot = volcano_plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```
